message: API credentials appear to be passed through a URL query string. Query strings are commonly logged by browsers, proxies, servers, and analytics tools.
fix: Move secrets to server-side environment variables and send credentials in secure headers or backend-only requests instead of URL parameters.

message: An API call uses plain HTTP. Data sent over HTTP can be intercepted or modified in transit.
fix: Use HTTPS API endpoints and reject insecure transport in production.

message: A sensitive-looking API route is exposed. Admin, debug, internal, or test endpoints are risky if deployed publicly.
fix: Protect sensitive endpoints with authentication and authorization, or remove them from production builds.

message: A debug or admin API appears to be enabled publicly.
fix: Disable debug/admin APIs by default and enable them only behind authenticated, role-checked access.

message: An API CORS setting allows any origin. This can let untrusted websites call your backend from a user's browser.
fix: Replace wildcard CORS with a short allowlist of trusted frontend domains.
